features.details: Search for and review the existing pull request associated with branch 'feat/issue-115-'. Determine its current status (open, closed, merged, draft), review state, and whether it properly addresses issue #115. Document findings.

Based on T001 findings, decide the appropriate action: if PR is valid and progressing, no action needed; if PR is stale/incomplete, either update it or close it and create a new one; if PR was merged but issue remains open, close the issue.

features.description: Update automation to check for existing PRs before creation
features.details: Modify the automation code that creates pull requests to first query GitHub API for existing open PRs on the target branch. If a PR already exists, either update the existing PR or skip creation with appropriate logging.

features.description: Add error handling for PR creation validation errors
features.details: Implement proper try-catch handling for GitHub API validation errors (422 status). When a 'pull request already exists' error occurs, log a warning, retrieve the existing PR details, and continue workflow gracefully instead of failing.

features.description: Add unit tests for duplicate PR scenarios
features.details: Create unit tests that verify the automation handles duplicate PR scenarios correctly. Test cases should include: existing open PR, existing merged PR, existing closed PR, and concurrent PR creation attempts.

Once the investigation is complete and automation improvements are in place, close this tracking issue with a comment explaining the resolution. Include reference to any PRs created or automation changes made.

overall_approach: 1) Investigate the existing pull request for issue #115 to determine its status. 2) Update the automation system to gracefully handle scenarios where a PR already exists. 3) Add proper error handling and recovery logic to prevent similar failures in the future. 4) Close the tracking issue once resolved.
potential_risks: The existing PR for issue #115 may be stale or in an inconsistent state
Automation changes could affect other workflows if not properly tested
Race conditions in automation could still cause duplicate PR attempts
Manual intervention might be needed for other similar failed automations
